site.title: Alexis Tacnet — Software Engineer, SRE and Designer

home.now.title: Now

home.projects.title: PROJECTS
home.projects.desc: I've always loved to work on projects on my own. Being able to ship something from start to end is something I'm really passionate about. A lot of projects did not make it to production, but some of them did.
home.libraries.title: LIBRARIES
home.libraries.desc: Helping the community by building open-source libraries is something I enjoy a lot. I try to build libraries that are useful and easy to use.
home.finance.title: FINANCE
home.finance.desc: I'm also passionate about finance and economics. I built some tools to help me understand the world better.
project.mots.desc: A portable dictionary to learn new words.
library.trpc-monitoring.desc: tRPC middleware to automatically add OTel and Logging of all procedures.
library.trpc-db.desc: tRPC collection for TanStack DB, enabling real time sync.
tool.buyvsrent.name: Buy vs rent
tool.buyvsrent.desc: In France, it is commonly admitted that renting a house is loosing a lot of money, but it's not always true. This is a tool to compare the cost of buying vs renting a house.
finance.buyvsrent.title: Buy vs Rent
finance.buyvsrent.subtitle: In France, it is commonly said that buying is more interesting than renting, notably because rent is "throwing money out the window". But the economic reality is more nuanced, as this comparison shows.
finance.buyvsrent.params.title: Parameters
finance.buyvsrent.params.subtitle: Your parameters for the simulation.
finance.buyvsrent.simulation.title: Simulation
finance.buyvsrent.simulation.subtitle: Comparison between buying and renting the property.
finance.buyvsrent.buy.title: Buy
finance.buyvsrent.buy.desc: Enter the information about the property purchase.
finance.buyvsrent.rent.title: Rent
finance.buyvsrent.rent.desc: Enter the information about the rental property.
finance.buyvsrent.inflation.title: Inflation rate
finance.buyvsrent.inflation.desc: Annual inflation rate affecting rents, property taxes, charges and maintenance.
finance.buyvsrent.inflation.label: Annual inflation rate
finance.buyvsrent.desc.buy.investment: The investment required to buy a property equals the savings invested in the property plus the costs related to the purchase.
finance.buyvsrent.desc.buy.costs: The costs of buying are mainly the loan interest, property tax, charges and maintenance.
finance.buyvsrent.desc.buy.savings: When buying, what you save is what you repay on the property purchase, known as the repaid principal.
finance.buyvsrent.desc.buy.gain: Capital (here the property) can generate a capital gain, more or less liquid. If real estate prices rise, the property value increases and there is a capital gain.
finance.buyvsrent.desc.buy.capital: In this comparison, what matters is the total capital (total savings) when buying versus renting.
finance.buyvsrent.desc.rent.investment: For renting, the investment is not calculated but corresponds to what one would have put into buying. If you have this investment capacity when buying, you have it when renting too.
finance.buyvsrent.desc.rent.costs: In the rental case, costs are only the rent, which may actually be more advantageous than buying.
finance.buyvsrent.desc.rent.savings: We assume that you save what you don't spend on rent.
finance.buyvsrent.desc.rent.gain: This savings can be invested in various vehicles, with varying returns.
finance.buyvsrent.desc.rent.capital: You can then compare the capital when buying with that when renting, which can actually be higher before a certain year!
finance.buyvsrent.tooltip.buy.costs.form: Costs = Interest paid + (Property tax + Charges + Maintenance) * Inflation
finance.buyvsrent.tooltip.buy.gain.formu: Capital gain = Property value * Capital gain rate
finance.buyvsrent.tooltip.buy.capital.fo: Total capital = Existing capital + Repaid principal + Capital gain
finance.buyvsrent.tooltip.rent.investmen: Investment = Same investment as when buying
finance.buyvsrent.tooltip.rent.gain.form: Capital gain = Existing capital * Investment rate
finance.buyvsrent.tooltip.rent.capital.f: Total capital = Existing capital + Savings + Capital gain
about.title: ABOUT ME
about.subtitle: Who am I and what I do.
about.whoami.title: Who am I
about.whoami.body: I'm Alexis Tacnet, a Software engineer, SRE and Designer based in Paris, France. I currently work at {company} as a Software Engineer.
about.swe.title: Software Engineer
about.swe.body: I've been programming since I'm 10 and I'm currently 29 years old. I started mainly with Web: HTML, CSS and a bit of early days JS, and then went with C and C++ for Desktop applications. I then switched to Android applications development in Java and published my first app to the Play Store ({androidMarket}) at 15. During my studies, I learned Python and experimented with Data Science / Web. I then went fullspeed towards Web with Python backend and JS front-end: Flask + Angular, Django + Vue, ... Nowadays, I'm mostly programming in Typescript.
about.sre.title: SRE
about.sre.body: As a teenager I discovered Linux with Ubuntu, and then ArchLinux. After a few failed installations and bad formatting of LUKS encrypted LVM partitions, I felt in love with self-hosting and managing unix servers. In my engineer school, I was the president of a student union managing the internet network on the campus. We were managing routers, fibers, ethernet cables and all the associated software: DHCP, DNS, Web hosting,... It was a really great experience that allowed me to work in startups to manage their VPS and soon clouds. After graduating, I cofounded a startup developing a Kubernetes manager to create and manage on-demand environments for web services.
about.designer.title: Designer
about.designer.body: I've always been passionate about design: from classical art to modern web design for Saas applications. I did not study in this field, and I was always frustrated that web developers need to rely on designers to make good products. After a lot of trial in game development on Inkscape when I was younger, I focused on new tools like Figma. I've always tried to make both brand assets and product design for my ideas. Currently, I'm not what I would be a good designer, but I try to improve to be as autonomous as possible to launch my ideas.
about.contact.title: Contact me
about.contact.body: You can contact me by sending an email to {email}, I usually respond in a few days.
about.findme.title: Where to find me
about.findme.body: You can also find me on other websites:
